channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Elon Musk is becoming a liability for his own businesses
item.description: SpaceX just pulled off an audacious, successful launch of its Starship rocket booster. The event, which occurred Sunday, is a massive moment for Elon Musk’s space exploration firm, and could kick-start the next phase of the space revolution.
item.dc:creator: Chris Stokel-Walker, Fast Company

item.title: RIP the Surface Laptop Go: Microsoft drops support for its budget laptop
item.description: After four years of software support, the Surface Laptop Go won’t be getting any more updates - and that could upset some users.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Windows users are being tricked by sneaky malware scheme
item.description: Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson reveals how security experts found new malware, Lumma Stealer, spreading via fake verification pages.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: AI being used to run world’s first indoor vertical berry farm
item.description: First indoor vertical berry farm opens in Virginia, using AI to grow 4 million pounds annually. Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson takes a closer look at the "growing" trend.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Aquona digitaliza sus operaciones en Castilla y León mediante la herramienta DROP
item.description: En el marco del proceso de transformación digital que está ejecutando Aquona, empresa responsable de la gestión del agua en más de 60 municipios de Castilla y León, se ha procedido a digitalizar la totalidad de sus operaciones dando un paso adelante en la mejora de la planificación de los trabajos y
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: How do we fund open source?
item.description: The world has come to rely upon the free work of millions of skilled software developers—the maintainers of free open-source software (FOSS) projects. But the world hasn’t given them a tip.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Murder trial of tech consultant in death of Cash App founder Bob Lee begins
item.description: The stabbing death of Cash App founder Bob Lee in San Francisco last year stunned the tech community.
item.dc:creator: Janie Har, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Volunteers bring solar power to Hurricane Helene's disaster zone
item.description: Nearly two weeks after Hurricane Helene downed power lines and washed out roads all over North Carolina's mountains, the constant din of a gas-powered generator is getting to be too much for Bobby Renfro.
item.dc:creator: GABRIELA AOUN ANGUEIRA, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: TikTok was aware of risks kids and teens face on its platform, legal document alleges
item.description: TikTok has been aware that its design features are detrimental to its young users and that publicly touted tools aimed at limiting kids’ time on the site were largely ineffective.
item.dc:creator: Haleluya Hadero, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: A whopping 342,817 Lego Technic pieces are used to build a sports car
item.description: Lego and McLaren, the British supercar manufacturer, teamed up to create a fully drivable, life-sized replica of the McLaren P1 hypercar.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 18 Android settings that’ll strengthen your security
item.description: You might not know it from all the panic-inducing headlines out there, but Android is actually packed with practical and powerful security options.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Marriott settles with the FTC for $52 million over data breaches
item.description: Marriott International has agreed to pay $52 million and make changes to bolster its data security to resolve state and federal claims related to major data breaches that affected more than 300 million of its customers worldwide.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Biometric data: Is it safe to hand it over to any company that asks?
item.description: Biometric data, like face and fingerprint scans, can help you save time when accessing your personal data – but with it comes a whole host of privacy risks.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: North Carolina maker of high-purity quartz back operating post-Helene
item.description: SPRUCE PINE, N.C. (AP) — One of the two companies that manufacture high-purity quartz used for making semiconductors and other high-tech products from mines in a western North Carolina community severely damaged by Hurricane Helene is operating again.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: One Tech Tip: Here's what you need to do before and after your phone is stolen or lost
item.description: Phones hold so much of our digital lives that if they ever get stolen or go missing, it can cause major disruption beyond just the loss of a device.
item.dc:creator: Kelvin Chan, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Microsoft unveils health care AI tools
item.description: Microsoft unveiled several new artificial intelligence (AI) tools on Thursday aiming to support health care organizations through medical imaging models, health care agent services and an AI-driven workflow solution for nurses.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Scully, The Hill

item.title: Astera Labs Unveils Industry's First PCIe 6 Fabric Switch for AI-Optimized Data Centers
item.description: Astera Labs introduces Scorpio, a PCIe 6 fabric switch designed for AI workloads in accelerated computing platforms. The switch aims to deliver maximum system utilization and uptime for scale-out PCIe 6 connectivity and scale-up GPU clustering in AI servers.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Say goodbye to dead batteries with this portable wind turbine
item.description: The Shine 2.0 is a 3-in-1 portable wind turbine, charge controller and power bank that fits into a backpack and works day and night, rain or shine.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: How to use Java generics to avoid ClassCastExceptions
item.description: Generics are used in Java to allow types or methods to operate on objects of various types while providing compile-time type safety. Generics address the problem of ClassCastExceptions being thrown at runtime as a result of code that is not type safe.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Planned Adobe app to put an end to AI training on images and clips
item.description: Adobe has unveiled plans for a new web app called Content Authenticity, which is part of the Content Credentials platform and will be used to ensure that AI models are not trained on content creators’ images, video, or audio clips without their permission.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: How to use your iPhone to get help by satellite
item.description: Apple’s decision to put satellite connections in iPhones is already saving lives, and as Hurricane Milton threatens Florida, now is the time for every US iPhone user to learn how the system works and why they should upgrade to iOS 18.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: TikTok is designed to be addictive to kids and causes them harm, US states' lawsuits say
item.description: More than a dozen states and the District of Columbia filed lawsuits against TikTok on Tuesday, saying that the popular short-form video app is designed to be addictive to kids and harms their mental health.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO and BARBARA ORTUTAY, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Federal Executive Forum Customer Experience Strategies in Government Progress and Best Practices 2024
item.description: Improving customer experience (CX) is a multi-faceted challenge to tackle as needs vary between stakeholders.
item.dc:creator: wfedstaff, Federal News Network
